subject: refactor: unify cancellation via generic _run_cancellable
body:
Replace model_turn's inline is_cancelled check and _execute_tool's
_run_with_cancellation with a single generic _run_cancellable wrapper
that handles both model streaming and tool execution uniformly.

- _run_with_cancellation(coro, canc) → _run_cancellable(coro, canc)
  with generic return type (was hard-coded to ToolResult)
- _model_turn drops its internal is_cancelled check; caller wraps it
  in _run_cancellable and catches ToolRunCancelled
- _execute_tool calls _run_cancellable internally (same behavior)
- _advance now catches ToolRunCancelled for model calls too (was
  only caught for tool execution)

subject: feat: add git tool for running git subcommands in workspace
body:
Introduce GitTool, a new built-in tool that executes git commands via
subprocess in the workspace directory. Wired into default_tools() so it
is available in every Run.

- GitTool accepts a `command` string (e.g. 'status', 'diff', 'log --oneline')
- Uses asyncio.create_subprocess_exec with sandbox.command_environment()
- 30s timeout, 128KB output limit with truncation, shlex token parsing
- 16 tests pass (4 new git-specific), ruff + mypy clean
prompt: add git tool for running git subcommands in workspace

subject: refactor: inject SandboxFactory into Harness; simplify SqliteCheckpointStore.claim
body:
- Harness now takes a SandboxFactory Protocol instead of hard-coding
  LocalSandbox at call sites, aligning with the other seams
- SqliteCheckpointStore.claim drops the redundant @contextmanager
  wrapper and returns RunLock.claim directly
prompt: inject SandboxFactory into Harness; simplify SqliteCheckpointStore.claim

subject: feat: resume without run_id defaults to the most recent Run
body:
- CLI resume command now accepts an optional run_id; when omitted,
  finds the last Run in the current workspace
- interactive /resume without a run id uses recover_run to attach
  to the workspace's most recent Run
prompt: resume without run_id defaults to the most recent Run

subject: refactor: simplify project config validation and address linting and formatting issues
prompt: simplify the workspace config loader and fix lint warnings

subject: refactor: parse .env with python-dotenv
body:
Replace the hand-rolled ~30-line .env parser with python-dotenv's
dotenv_values(), removing parsing code we maintained ourselves and
gaining its broader, well-tested .env handling. The real-environment
precedence (including empty-string overrides) is preserved in _get.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

subject: refactor: share the missing-configuration error message
body:
doctor and _render_configuration_error rendered the same message and
env-var hint independently, differing only by a trailing "again".
Parametrize the helper and call it from both, so the wording and
variable names live in one place.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>

subject: fix: let an empty real env var override a .env value
body:
_get used `os.environ.get(key) or dotenv.get(key)`, so a variable set to
an empty string in the real environment fell through to a stale .env
value, contradicting the documented "prefer the real environment"
precedence. Treat a key present in os.environ as authoritative even when
empty, so an explicit empty override resolves to the default.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
prompt: let an empty real env var override a .env value

subject: fix: let init create a missing workspace instead of crashing
body:
init used resolve(strict=True), so `milky-frog init ./newproject` on a
not-yet-existing path raised a raw FileNotFoundError traceback. Resolve
the path non-strictly and create the workspace with parents=True, and
render a clean error with exit code 1 for genuine filesystem problems
(e.g. a workspace path that is actually a file).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
prompt: let init create a missing workspace instead of crashing

subject: feat: honor max_model_calls from project config.toml
body:
config.toml was written by init but never read, and the value 30 was
duplicated across the inline init template, the committed
.milky-frog/config.toml, and RunRequest's default. Add a project-config
loader that reads <workspace>/.milky-frog/config.toml and apply its
max_model_calls to each Run, with a single DEFAULT_MAX_MODEL_CALLS
constant as the one source of truth. Malformed or missing config falls
back to the default instead of raising.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
prompt: read the per-workspace config file and apply its turn limit to each Run

subject: perf: build the runtime once and reuse it across Runs
body:
MilkyFrog previously rebuilt the model, AsyncOpenAI client, tool
registry, checkpoint store, and harness on every run() call, so the
interactive loop paid full setup/teardown each turn. Assemble the
harness once in __init__ and drive Runs through a single persistent
event loop, so the model's connection pool is reused instead of being
rebound to a fresh, immediately-closed loop per call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
prompt: assemble the execution harness once at startup instead of rebuilding per call

subject: fix: keep interactive loop alive when a task is cancelled
body:
Ctrl+C during a running task now cancels just that task and returns to
the prompt, instead of exiting the whole REPL. A second Ctrl+C at the
empty prompt still exits as before.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 <noreply@anthropic.com>
prompt: let Ctrl+C cancel just the current task and return to the prompt
